title: cookie-session with httpOnly: false — the session cookie is readable from JavaScript (XSS session theft)

params.passDetail: cookie-session sets httpOnly: true — the session cookie is not exposed to document.cookie and cannot be read by client-side scripts.
params.failDetail: cookie-session is configured with httpOnly: false, exposing the signed session cookie to JavaScript via document.cookie. Any XSS on the origin can read and exfiltrate the session and hijack the user's account. Set httpOnly: true (the default) for session cookies.
params.absentCallDetail: No cookieSession() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: cookieSession() does not set httpOnly; the default (true) applies — verify it is not overridden elsewhere.

provenance.note: Distinct from cookie-session-secure-false: httpOnly: false is set so front-end JS can read the session, trading the primary XSS-session-theft mitigation for convenience. The app keeps working, so it ships silently.
